animals.Name: Twinkle Toes
animals.Description:
Meet Twinkle Toes. A 7 year old Pittie x lab mix with great house manners and a zest for life. She came to us after being found as a stray in Texas and as soon as you meet her you can tell that she was a well-loved pet. She knows all her basic tricks, doesn't counter surf, and hasn't had a single potty accident with us. She's matured out her puppy energy and just wants a warm home and a best friend to play and go on adventures with.

Her daily routine:

7 am - she dozes away in her dog bed on the floor until I am awake then she patiently waits for me to take her outside by resting her chin on the edge of her bed, staring at me, and wagging her tail. She goes outside for a potty, eats her breakfast, and goes on a walk (10-30 min).

9 am - she stays in her room while I go to work for the day. She's stayed home up to 9 hours outside of her kennel without any potty or chewing incidents.

6 pm - She goes for another walk (10-30 min) and eats dinner.

7 pm - She loves to play with her favorite toy, Froggie. Tug, catch, or just squeaking away on her own. When she's done playing or I have to rescue Froggie from a squeaker-ectomy she curls up in her bed and relaxes for the rest of the night.

10 pm - she has her last potty break and goes to sleep on her bed in my room.

Training:
Twinkle Toes knows the basics: sit, stay, lie down, come, paw, and speak. She's food motivated and can learn new tricks with a bit of patience. She learned to trim her own nails with a scratch board in just a couple of days. She's still working on her leash manners and at the beginning of her walks she usually pulls to explore some sniffs or chase a squirrel. Once she gets some of her excitement out she settles down and walks much more calmly by your side.

Twinkle Toes is wary of strangers and other dogs on leash. She handles moderate foot traffic just fine — it's when people make direct eye contact or approach her that she gets uncomfortable and may bark or growl. She's not aggressive; she's just telling you she needs space. Other dogs on walks are best observed from across the street. With a calm handler who knows how to read the environment and advocate for her, she does great.

Twinkle Toes can get a little too excited sometimes and would prefer a home without cats, rabbits, or other small critters.

If you want to adopt a dog but don't want to start at square one, Twinkle Toes might be perfect for you. Don't let her silver muzzle fool you, Twinkle Toes is young at heart and has plenty of energy and adventures left in her.

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Demo
animals.Description:
Demo is a 1-year-old mixed-breed dog, possibly a Pyrenees mix. He comes to us from our Alabama partner, where he was found as a stray.  He had minimal human interaction while being a stray. Because of this, Demo needs a patient owner who will let him warm up on his own terms. Demo loves other dogs and is truly a dog’s dog.

Energy Level:
Demo is a low- to medium-energy pup who loves to play with all the dogs in his foster home for an hour or two at night (or longer if there’s a high-energy player in the house). He will play whenever another dog wants to, but he is very respectful if they don’t. He is happy to go on a daily walk if you’d like, but he can also take a day off from walking.

Training:
Demo has mostly been learning how to live in a house while in Wisconsin and that his foster mom and visitors are safe. Demo is a major creature of habit. He runs to his kennel for meals. He waits on the deck for the resident dog to go outside with him so they can leap off the steps at the same time and run to the back corner of the yard. Did I mention that he loves other dogs?

Demo also loves scent work and he's really good at it!  We are only working with one scent right now, vanilla.  This is a great training game for him to gain confidence in working with his humans!

Crate Training:
Demo is very food motivated and goes into his kennel easily because he knows he gets meals and treats there. 

Potty Training:
Demo is fully potty trained.

Grooming:
Demo has hair similar to a Lab and will need regular brushing due to shedding.

Dogs:
Demo loves every dog in his home. He also goes to doggy daycare and is making new friends there. When he sees another dog across the street while on a walk, he completely ignores them.

Cats:
Demo lives with two cats. They mostly stay upstairs, but when he is around them he gets a bit excited and wants to be best friends. He doesn’t understand that when they run away, they are trying to get away from him. Demo is an aggressive sniffer, so cats that don’t like being sniffed by a dog are not a good match for him.

Update: Demo sleeps upstairs in the bedroom that the cats are in at night with the resident dog and his foster mom to help him bond.  He’s doing excellent at learning good behaviors with the cats.  He can still get a little nosy though because he loves everyone.

Kids:
Demo gets nervous around kids that are the same height as him and will drool, cower and shy away. He has not met teens in my presence.

People:
When Demo meets new people, he follows the resident dog and mirrors their behavior. He is gaining confidence and learning a lot. Demo will lie on the couch to hang out with his foster mom and sometimes even snuggles. He loves being on the bed and now allows you to snuggle with him and he’ll even put his face against yours to snuggle in.  He’s gaining so much confidence and trust!  He’s even starting to let his foster mom pet him while walking by in the house.

Overall, Demo is an incredibly sweet and goofy boy. He wants everyone to like him but is a little shy at first. Once you are fully accepted into his pack, Demo will sit on you—or another dog. He would do best in a home with another confident dog to learn from, and even better if that dog enjoys playing!

animals.Name: Wish
animals.Description:
When you Wish upon a star...you will meet the sweetest princess you have ever met. With her beautiful black coat with white markings and her soulful eyes, she will melt your heart. Her tail is always wagging and she turns her body sideways while she is doing it. Mornings are her favorite time of day as everything is brand new again.

Wish is a true Southern belle who enjoys running around my fenced in yard whether she has the zoomies or you’re throwing a ball. I call her my little lady. She has learned to love the snow and will pick up pieces and throw them around or eat them. I keep telling her she doesn't need to do that as there is a pail of water in the house.

We’ve been working on sit, touch, look, off, leave it, and quiet. She has the sit, touch and look down pat but still needs guidance for off, leave it, and quiet. I have no doubt she will understand that with time. She is very treat motivated and sits quietly waiting for her breakfast or dinner. She is a younger dog, probably about two so consistent training will be the key to her success. Perhaps a basic training class to help her learn and provide additional socialization will help her reach her full potential.

She is super sweet, a little shy, loving and snuggly and will follow you around the house. She’s also playful and enjoys walks (still learning leash walking) and toys. She loves car rides and sits quietly looking around. She has no issues being in her crate and willingly goes in there before I even show her a treat. She is good for about eight hours so I do have a friend come and let her out while I am at work.

She gets along well with my resident dogs and has met a few others, too. She loves kids and is very gentle and patient with them. I have not found her to be afraid of storms, loud noises, or even the vacuum cleaner although she wants to attack it. When I redirect her from something I don't want her to do to something I do want her to do I simply say Yes and give her a treat to let her know that is what I want. She is great at puzzle games and loves her snuffle mat and lick mat.

Wish recently completed heartworm treatment and her restrictions were lifted, meaning she can go back to being a dog. We are slowly building up her tolerance to exercise and playing. She enjoys having a fenced in backyard as it gives her the freedom to do zoomies (not right now of course), sniff in the yard, and basically be an honest to goodness dog.

If you believe that wishing on a star will make your dreams come true, then come meet Wish...Are you the one to make her dreams come true?

animals.Name: Smilie
animals.Description:
Meet Smilie! He's a chill affectionate big lug of a companion that will warm your heart with his big smiles and calm demeanor. He's crate trained and potty trained but can also free roam. He can be stubborn but does know sit and down.

Smilie would do well in a home that has a fenced yard but would also likely do well in an apartment. He is pretty quiet but will bark to alert you to things.

He would do best in an adult home or with older kids as he struggled with young children in his previous home. He has met many children at events and done very well with those encounters.

He is also dog selective and prefers more mellow dogs. He has gotten along very well with our resident yellow lab mix who is fairly relaxed but they also enjoy wrestling together.

Once you meet Smilie, you'll be in love with him.

animals.Name: Jane
animals.Description:
Meet Jane! This sweet 3-year-old momma has already raised 11 adorable puppies, and now it’s finally her turn to find a loving forever home. Jane is a wonderful mix of playful energy and couch-potato charm—she’ll happily romp around outside, explore new places, and then curl up beside you for some serious snuggle time. 

Jane is a smart cookie who loves learning new things and enjoys playtime with other dogs, but she’d prefer to be the only dog in the home so she can soak up all the love and attention. Because of her playful style, she would do best in a household with children 12 or older. At about 50 pounds of pure sweetness, Jane is the perfect “play hard, snuggle hard” companion. If you’re looking for a loyal friend who will make you laugh, keep you active, and cuddle up at the end of the day, Jane might just be your girl. Ready to give this deserving mama her happily-ever-after? Jane can’t wait to meet you!

animals.Name: Buddy
animals.Description:
Buddy is a senior cocker spaniel who was surrendered to Fetch when his elderly owner developed health issues and had to go into long term care. He is a happy guy with a big heart, a touch of doggy dementia and the bladder of a polite grandpa.

Buddy absolutely loves other dogs and wants to be near them all the time. He is hard of hearing and doesn’t always notice when other dogs growl at him or don’t want his affection, and he can be a bit persistent with his love, but he responds well if another dog very firmly tells him to leave them alone. Buddy loves his people very much and is always up for attention and snuggles. He is not so good with strangers and will bark loudly when he sees someone he doesn’t know. Sometimes he forgets that he has already met someone and will bark loudly again. He is not a bite risk as he only has a few teeth left (more on that later) but I typically put him in his crate when new people come over so he doesn’t get worked up.

Buddy has some health issues typical of senior dogs. He has a heart murmur that does not currently cause any symptoms and some arthritis that does not keep him from loving his walks and pulling hard on his leash. He has been diagnosed with chronic stenosis of the ear canals so will need to be monitored for ear infections. He also has KCS which is common in smaller breeds and means his eyes do not produce enough tears. He is on an eye ointment twice a day to stimulate tear production and his eyes look perfect now, but he will need to stay on the eye ointment for the rest of his life. He had most of his teeth removed due to severe dental disease, but he still prefers hard kibble to softened food. He can’t chew but he just swallows it whole.

Buddy is mostly good in his crate but he will bark randomly if he wants attention or if he needs to potty, which is about every 2-3 hours during the day. He sleeps in his crate for 9-10 hours at night without a problem. As long as he is let out frequently during the day, he does not have accidents.

If you have a spot in your home for a fuzzy old man who will bark loudly at you for about 10 minutes and then love you forever, come meet Buddy!

animals.Name: Jubilee
animals.Description:
Jubilee is growing up fast. She is potty trained, walks confidently on a leash, sleeps all night in her crate, and is fully recovered from getting spayed. This resilient and joyful girl continues to prove that being blind doesn't slow her down one bit. She has blossomed into a brave, curious pup who navigates her world with confidence and a wagging tail.

Jubilee is a true social butterfly. She absolutely loves meeting new people and greets every new dog or cat she encounters with enthusiasm. She thrives in a fenced yard where she can safely explore, play with her toys, and keep herself busy with puppy "zoomies."

During the day, she is equally happy to relax in her crate or follow around her humans and plop on the floor wherever they can be found. She's a bit of a lazy bones, always saving up energy for the next adventure or homecoming.

Jubilee is very "pro-pet" and wants to be friends with everyone.She is good with other dogs and enjoys their company, although being blind, she does not pick up on body language. She is best suited for a dog that is tolerant of puppy energy.

As for felines, Jubilee is "cat-curious" and can live successfully with feline friends who know how to keep their distance. Because she cannot see social cues, she is currently struggling to learn physical boundaries with her foster's resident cats. She would do best in a home with "dog-savvy" cats or a family willing to help her navigate these introductions with patience and a few baby gates!

Special Needs Note

Jubilee was born with congenital retinal atrophy, but she doesn't know she's different. Adopting a blind puppy like Jubilee is an incredibly rewarding experience. It builds a unique bond of trust and communication. With a consistent environment and a little guidance, she will continue to thrive and inspire everyone she meets.

animals.Name: Duke
animals.Description:
Meet Duke, a handsome adolescent cattle dog mix with a big personality and the most perfect ears. Duke loves everyone he’s met. He welcomes guests into our house and immediately asks for pets. He also currently lives with two dogs and loves to play with the younger one.

Smart and extremely food-motivated, Duke is easy and fun to train. He apparently thinks kibble is the greatest thing that exists in this world. He has some classic cattle dog traits and adolescent behaviors, so he won’t be the right fit for every home, but for the right family or active individual, he’s an amazing companion.

A day in Duke’s life:

Morning: 45-minute walk to start the day

Playtime with resident dog, then breakfast

Crated during the workday

Evening: yard play, then free in the house with occasional playtime

Dinner

Night: a chew and cozy hanging-out time

Duke’s ideal home is active and consistent, and can provide daily exercise, mental stimulation, and continued training. Duke needs a home that’s more experienced with dogs of his breed mix and age, who can continue to guide him toward becoming a thoughtful adult who makes his own good choices. He can get overexcited when seeing dogs, but would love to live with compatible dogs after a proper introduction and if the dogs are consistently separated for food, as he can guard meals and chews. If you’re looking for a loyal, smart, and affectionate buddy and are ready for a lively young cattle-dog mix, Duke could be your perfect match.

animals.Name: Diamond
animals.Description:
Meet Princess Diamond: A Snuggly, Dazzling Girl Looking for Her Forever Home!

Diamond is one of the most affectionate dogs you’ll be lucky to meet. Though she needs patient introductions to new humans, she adores every human her foster family has brought into her circle! Diamond is a cuddly girl, and loves to lay on top of you or by your feet. Your heart will melt when she rests her chin on your chest.  She will bond deeply with whoever is lucky enough to adopt her.

Rescued in Arkansas, Diamond spent several months with a local rescue before making her way to the Midwest. She’s adapted exceptionally well to Wisconsin living!

Diamond is polite and relaxed inside her home. She rarely barks, never chews on items she shouldn’t, and loves to laze around in her favorite cozy spots! She has never shown any kind of resource guarding with humans, and is gentle when taking treats. She loves toys and will happily play fetch or tug with her humans.

Diamond is fully potty-trained and crate-trained. She’s never had an accident inside the house. Diamond is a smart girl and will go into her crate on command! She will happily snooze in her crate for up to 4 hours at a time. Diamond can also be left to free-roam.

Diamond walks well on leash, and prefers leisurely strolls with lots of sniffing and joyful rolling in the grass. She’s a curious girl, and will occasionally lunge at a squirrel or bird. Diamond is working through leash reactivity towards other pups, but can be redirected with treats.

Because of her past behavior towards other dogs, Diamond must be the only pet in her home. This is ideal so she can give you all her love and attention!

Diamond would do well in an adult-only home or in a home with older, quieter kids.

Diamond is looking for a loving adopter who is willing to take things slowly as she adjusts to her new home. If you're the kind of person who appreciates a sweet, affectionate dog with a big heart, Diamond is the dog for you.

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Joyce
animals.Description:
Joyce is an active, curious little girl who loves tug toys, wrestling with dog friends, and exploring the world around her. Joyce is incredibly social with other dogs and does a great job reading their signals and respecting boundaries.

She recently met a toddler and handled it beautifully, showing off her sweet and gentle side. Like many herding mixes, Joyce does have some classic puppy herding instincts and will happily grab at pant legs when she’s excited and playing.

She’s catching on quickly to potty training and is already sleeping well in her crate for about six hours overnight. Joyce can take a little time to settle down after playtime, but once she does, she’s content and happy.

With a mom around 25 pounds, Joyce will likely stay on the smaller side. She’s fun, affectionate, smart, and full of personality — the kind of puppy who keeps you smiling every day.

animals.Name: Inoha
animals.Description: Inoha is a cuddler. She has settled in to routine. She is great in her crate and has been going potty outside pretty consistently. She is a gem of a pup.

animals.Name: Rocco
animals.Description: Rocco is the steady, self‑assured pup of the group. He’s brave, curious, and always the first to check out something new. He loves playtime, training, and being near his people. Rocco will grow into a loyal, confident companion who thrives with structure and engagement.

animals.Name: Jaxen
animals.Description: Jaxen is laid‑back, curious, and adaptable. He enjoys adventure but doesn’t need to be the center of attention. He’s the pup who happily trots along, takes everything in, and then settles down for a nap. Jaxen will be a great fit for a family looking for a balanced, friendly companion.

animals.Name: Lilly
animals.Description:
Lilly is a tall, elegant, 50+ lb foxhound with a heart of gold and legs that seem made for flying. She showed up as a stray on January 6 with two other hounds, all in rough shape but unbelievably sweet—and since then, she’s blossomed into a playful, affectionate companion. Lilly still has that classic hound energy and would thrive with daily walks, hikes, or playtime (plus maybe doggy daycare a few times a week), but she’s learned to settle and loves her naps just as much as her zoomies. She’s especially famous for her joyful “parkour” moves—sprinting full speed and leaping over her dog friends mid-run like a seasoned athlete. A secure fenced yard is a must, as her nose will happily lead her on miles-long adventures if given the chance.

More than anything, Lilly is pure love. Sit on the floor and she’ll be in your lap in seconds; let her on the bed and she’ll curl right into you, happiest when she’s as close as possible. She adores people (not exactly watchdog material 😉) and has no signs of aggression, though her enthusiastic greetings and ongoing manners training mean she may do best in a home without small children. She’s made huge progress with house training—she even has an adorable “wiggle dance” to tell you she needs to go out—and sleeps beautifully through the night, whether on her bed or yours. Lilly does best with companionship and is still building confidence being alone, but with continued support she’s on a great path. She’s a loving, loyal hound who will reward her future family with endless affection—and yes, plenty of cuddles.

animals.Name: Nellie
animals.Description:
Nellie is a gentle, soulful foxhound who’s made an incredible journey from scared and fragile to quietly brave and loving. When she was rescued, she was underweight, injured, and too frightened to trust—but with patience, she’s grown into a healthy 50-pound girl who now seeks out affection and melts into her people 🧡. She can still be a little shy at first, but once she feels safe, her sweet personality shines through in the softest, most heart-melting ways.
She loves simple things: long sniffy walks, fresh air, and being near her humans. A secure fence is a must (her nose will lead her on adventures!), and she’s still building confidence being alone, but she’s making steady progress. Nellie doesn’t ask for much—just love and patience—but in return, she gives a deep, loyal bond and the kind of quiet companionship that feels really special.

animals.Name: Sally
animals.Description: Sally is love with a hint of lime 🍋‍🟩🐾 💚. She is a petite foxhound, about 35 pounds, estimated to be a little over a year old. And while she is the consummate snuggle bug, don’t let her size and sweetness fool you! She is a problem solver through and through: her sweet adorable brain never stops puzzling - you can almost see her little gears turning while she tries to figure out her next big move. It makes sense – not only does her breeding lend itself to independent thinking, but her size and, likely the conditions she was born into, have required her to use that gift to full advantage. And so it is that Sally figures out how to get what she needs in life: whether it’s pets and cuddles, an extra treat or an escape hatch under the fence 😳. She does it all so totally and completely adorably (her secret weapon), that it is impossible to be upset with her...and the truth is that for Sally, life up to this point has been about survival. Like her two packmates who she was rescued with, she continues to work through hypervigilance to noises, people carrying things, taking food from a human hand and being left home alone. That said, there is just something about Sally, some little extra flame of playful curiosity that might either get her into trouble, OR…you just never know, with enough design and determination, might just land her the biggest prize in the whole shootin’ match 🧡

animals.Name: Pinkie
animals.Description:
Meet your new favorite little shadow 💛
This 11-pound Chihuahua is all parts adorable. She is a reserved lady and takes some time to get comfortable with new people. Once she does, her heart is yours.  She’s already 100% house trained and doing well with crate training. She cries a little at the beginning, but is quiet and comfortable for the rest of the day.  She’s happiest close to her people—whether that’s snuggling on the couch or quietly keeping you company around the house.
She does best with older, respectful kids (12+) and is tolerant of calm, well-mannered dogs. Our family absolutely adores her, and it’s easy to see why—she’s sweet, funny and full of charm in a teeny tiny little package. Pinkie does have a history of a positive heartworm test and has received treatment. It will be especially important for her new family to be committed to every month, year-round heartworm prevention and appropriate follow-up veterinary care. 
If you’re looking for a loyal little companion who’s already got the basics down, she might just be your perfect match. We just love her and want nothing but the best!

animals.Name: Kai
animals.Description:
Kai is my charming little rogue. Sweet, but a little mischievous, he loves to explore for things to chew on (his favorite is the door stopper), but loves to crawl in your lap and lick your nose even more! A total heartbreaker. 

Brother to Deva, Lucas, and Nola!

animals.Name: Deva
animals.Description:
A fiery redhead that’s true to her name, Deva is the star, the big sister, and the representative of her siblings.  She’s brave, adventurous, and not afraid to tell you what she thinks. When her big sister work is done, she's in your lap being the perfect cuddle bug.

Sister to Nola, Lucas, and Kai!

animals.Name: Nola
animals.Description:
Nola is a total sweetheart. Always first out of the gate to give you some loving, she's a silly, sweet, playful girl.  If she's not in cuddling up with you, she's pouncing on a toy or (preferably) on her siblings!

Sister to Deva, Kai, and Lucas!

animals.Name: Huck
animals.Description:
Meet Huck — Fetch's 5000th rescue! He is a big, smushy lug of a pup who’s all heart and curiosity. He has settled in beautifully with his foster family and loves being part of the pack.



Personality: Affectionate, playful, and easygoing. Huck adores cuddles and will flop at your feet for belly rubs between bursts of puppy energy. He enjoys exploring the yard — digging in the garden, chewing sticks, and chasing birds — and happily tags along when the other dogs run the yard, even if he’s not sure why. 


Training status: Quick learner who responds well to corrections and positive reinforcement. Potty-trained, crate-trained and sleeps well through the night. Currently working on basic commands, learning his name, and not jumping up on people. 

Daily routine: Mornings and evenings include walks and yard time for exploration and play. During the day he enjoys supervised outdoor time, naps, and social time with his foster siblings. Huck will also happily join you for a jog! Regular exercise followed by cuddle sessions keeps him content.

Social skills:Huck LOVES people. If you are a human willing to pet him, he will love you forever. He is also excellent with other dogs and is learning a lot from his foster brothers. He is calm and mostly indifferent to a newborn baby — a promising sign for family life. 


Quirks & needs: Huck is a typical puppy — still learning boundaries and manners. He teases with puppy nibbles while teething but accepts his teether toy readily. He’ll need patient, consistent correction and continued training to reinforce good behavior.



Best fit home: An active, patient home that can offer regular walks, yard time, structured training, and lots of affection. A household with other friendly dogs and/or someone able to work on boundaries and alone-time training will be ideal. Huck is a goofy, loyal companion who gives big love — worth the puppy work for a lifetime of laughs and cuddles.

animals.Name: Maya
animals.Description: May 4, 2026Meet Maya, a 51-pound "lap dog" who hasn’t quite looked at a tape measure lately! At three years old, this Australian Kelpie mix is the perfect blend of sweetness and soul.Maya is the definition of "medium energy"—she’s always up for an adventurous neighborhood stroll to take in the sights and smells, but she’s just as happy to clock out for a professional-grade snuggle session. Despite her size, her favorite place in the world is right on top of you; she’s a world-class cuddler who truly believes she fits perfectly in any lap.If you’re looking for a lovable, amazing companion who is as gentle as she is loyal, Maya is ready to move right in and steal your heart (and your seat on the couch).

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Natalia
animals.Description:
Meet Natalia, the hound mix with floppy ears, a curious nose, and a heart full of sunshine. She wakes up every morning ready for joy - whether that means exploring the world with her puppy enthusiasm or curling up for a well‑earned nap afterwards.
Natalia is the perfect mix of playful energy and gentle sweetness. She loves toys, treats, belly rubs, and anyone willing to be her friend. She’s smart, eager to learn, and already working hard on her puppy manners. With a little guidance and a lot of love, she’ll grow into the loyal, loving companion she’s meant to be.
Thoughtful and affectionate, Natalia takes in the world with wide, wondering eyes. She enjoys walks with lots of sniffs and quiet moments just as much as she loves making people smile.
If you’re looking for a pup who brings joy, warmth, and a whole lot of heart into your home, Natalia is ready to trot her way into your life.

animals.Name: Marigold
animals.Description: Marigold was surrendered from an Amish farm where she was used for breeding. She is shy but very sweet and will wag her tail at everyone and cautiously come up for pets. She is learning housetraining and walking on a leash. She loves other dogs and is very friendly and submissive with my pack. Marigold is a nonshedding dog and will need regular haircuts. She was very matted when we picked her up and had to be completely shaved, but she will be very cute and fluffy when her hair grows out! She is a rare straight-haired bichon, not the typical curly coat.

animals.Name: Chloe
animals.Description:
Chloe was surrendered from an Amish farm where she was used for breeding. She is the sweetest little thing but is very shy. She will wag her tail at us from a few feet away but crouch on the ground when we lean to pet her. She is much more used to other dogs than people, and has loved every dog she has met. She is very good in a crate but is new to living in a house so we are still working on leash and potty training. She has not mastered stairs yet.

Chloe is a rare straight-haired bichon. She is non-shedding but does not have the typical curly bichon coat. She was very matted when we picked her up and had to be completely shaved, but she will be so cute when her hair grows out!

animals.Name: Max
animals.Description:
Max is a beautiful 2 year old Siberian husky who was surrendered due to his owner’s health issues. He is a happy, friendly guy who is potty trained, crate trained and knows basic commands (although whether he chooses to listen or is distracted by his husky brain is another story lol - but if you have food, he will ALWAYS listen!). He gets along great with bigger dogs but gets a little too excited around small dogs and cats, so I have limited his exposure to them as they are scared of him. Max is very chill for a husky and spends most of the day sleeping, although he does enjoy going out in the yard and exploring the fence line. He loves to get pets and rub up against you. If he wants attention he will talk to you as huskies do until you notice him.

Max has idiopathic epilepsy and takes two seizure medications. It is important that he take these medications on time to keep him seizure free. He has pet insurance from his previous owner that can be passed on to his adopter.

If you are looking for a dog who is past the puppy stage but still young enough to be your buddy for many years, Max is your guy!

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Tigger
animals.Description:
Meet Tigger — or as he would say, “T-I-double-guh-er! That spells Tigger!” 🐾

This 7-month-old Saint Bernard puppy absolutely lives up to his name with his goofy, bouncy personality. Tigger loves playing with our resident Bernese Mountain Dog and is happiest when he has a dog buddy to romp around with. Whether he’s tossing toys around by himself, chewing on a teething toy, or giving you a cheerful “Hoo-hoo-hoo-hoo!”, he’s always finding ways to keep life fun.

Tigger has a nice medium energy level for a giant-breed puppy. He enjoys 1–2 daily walks plus playtime with other dogs, but he’s also content entertaining himself and settling down for snuggles afterward. He rides well in the car, walks nicely on leash, sleeps quietly in his crate at night, and can stay crated during the workday without being destructive or vocal.

Like many young dogs, Tigger is still learning about the world. He can be nervous around strangers and may bark when meeting new people, but he’s been making good progress with socialization and will need a patient adopter who can continue building his confidence. He’s also working through some separation anxiety and wants to be greeted with cuddles when his owner returns home, so consistency and routine will help him thrive. 

One of Tigger’s funny quirks is that he tends to drink a LOT of water at once — which means he’ll usually need a couple potty breaks afterward. A home ready for typical puppy management and continued training will set him up for success.

Tigger would do best in a home that enjoys a playful, affectionate giant puppy and ideally has another friendly dog for him to “consider yourself pounced!” with. If you’re looking for a lovable goofball with a big heart, soft cuddles, and plenty of personality, Tigger may be your perfect match.
“TTFN! Ta-ta for now!” — Tigger

animals.Name: Gilmore
animals.Description:
Gilmore is the ultimate lover boy. He is always up for a cuddle, kiss, or belly rub. He loves humans and wants to be held or snuggled up. He does enjoy dogs and wants to play, but is learning appropriate play manners.

Gilmore does very well in his kennel, but sometimes needs a noise machine (he is currently being fostered in an apartment, and sometimes the outside noise he thinks is his people coming home).  Gilmore loves the outdoors, but is quick to need a rest after playing.  He loves sticks and chasing birds.

Gilmore would be the perfect fit for any family.  He would do great as the only dog, or with other dogs.  He would do great with children, or just adults.  He is flexible as long as the people around him love him as much as he loves them.

Gilmore is a little warrior with a heart that could light up a whole room. At just under 3 months old, this boy has already proven he’s tougher than he looks — he fought his way through parvo and came out the other side with a wagging tail and a joyful spirit.

Despite his rough start, Gilmore is pure sweetness. He approaches life with gentle curiosity, soft eyes, and a puppy wiggle that could melt anyone. He loves being held, loves exploring safe spaces, and loves curling up for naps like he’s making up for lost time. His resilience shows in the way he greets every new moment with trust and hope.

Gilmore’s personality is tender and bright:
• affectionate and people‑oriented
• playful in short, happy bursts
• friendly with other gentle pets
• eager to learn and be part of a routine

He’s ready for a home that will cherish him, protect him, and celebrate the brave little pup he is. Gilmore is working on potty training, enjoys soft toys, and thrives on calm encouragement and cuddles.

If you’re looking for a puppy with a survivor’s heart and a gentle, loving soul, Gilmore is ready to begin his new life — one filled with comfort, kindness, and a family who sees how special he truly is.

animals.Name: Elver
animals.Description:
Elver is strong, brave, and already a little legend. At just under 3 months old, he has overcome more in his short life than most pups ever will — including surviving his bout of parvo. And he didn’t just pull through… he bounced back with the sweetest, happiest spirit you could imagine.

Elver is a survivor in every sense, but you’d never know he had a rough start. He greets every day with a wagging tail, bright eyes, and a whole lot of puppy enthusiasm. He loves playtime, soft toys, and curling up against you like he’s finally found the safe place he always deserved.

His personality is pure sunshine:
• incredibly affectionate
• eager to learn
• gentle with people and other pets
• and full of that goofy Boxer‑mix charm

Elver is ready to start the next chapter of his life — one filled with love, stability, and a family who sees how special he truly is. He’s working on potty training, enjoys routine, and thrives on encouragement and snuggles.

If you’re looking for a puppy with a courageous heart, a sweet soul, and a whole lifetime of love ahead of him, Elver is ready to meet you. He’s proof that the smallest fighters often become the biggest blessings.

animals.Name: Stacy (bonded to Ruthie)
animals.Description:
Bonded to Ruthie: https://fetchwi.org/dog#ruthie

Sweet Stacy is the definition of gentle soul energy.
This shy little lady wants love so badly — she’s just still figuring out how to trust that it’s really hers to have. Stacy is incredibly sweet, easygoing, and wonderfully quirky in all the best ways. She gets along beautifully with basically every furry creature she meets and approaches life with quiet softness and polite submission.
Thunderstorms? No big deal.
Doorways? Absolutely suspicious.
Stacy often looks to Ruthie for guidance on “how to dog,” and watching the two together is enough to melt anyone’s heart. Ruthie gives Stacy confidence, and Stacy gives Ruthie comfort — they truly are a package deal of love and emotional support.
Once Stacy feels safe, her affectionate side starts to shine. She enjoys attention, seeks reassurance from her people, and blossoms most in calm, patient homes that let her move at her own pace.
Stacy is looking for:
A patient adopter who understands shy dogs
A calm, predictable environment
Her beloved Ruthie by her side
Someone willing to cheer her on through scary things… like doorways
Behind Stacy’s timid exterior is an incredibly loving girl who just needs someone to believe in her.

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Ruthie (bonded to Stacy)
animals.Description:
Bonded with Stacy: https://fetchwi.org/dog#stacy

Meet Ruthie — the wiggliest girl in town! This sweet mama greets life with a tail that spins in circles like a tiny belly dancer, and she fully believes every human exists to provide belly rubs, snacks, and compliments. Honestly? She may be right.
Ruthie is a happy, affectionate girl who loves walks, loves people, loves other dogs, and really loves playing with her best friend and sister-from-another-mister, Stacy. Earlier this year, Ruthie proudly raised our adorable pasta puppy litter, and she’s now ready for a chapter that’s all about her.
She’s eager to please, enjoys learning, and has the kind of goofy charm that makes everyone fall in love fast. Ruthie has been treated for heartworm and is feeling better every day.
And yes… before you ask… Stacy’s mom does have it going on, because these two are deeply bonded and adore each other.
Ruthie’s ideal home will have:
A secure fenced yard for zoomies and sunbathing
Plenty of snacks and belly rubs

If you’re looking for a joyful, loving dog with endless personality and a built-in best friend, Ruthie is ready to wiggle her way into your heart.

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Cash
animals.Description:
Good with dogs, Good with kids, Good with Cats, House trained, Can free roam, Walks well on leash, Good in car, Senior Dog, Crate Trained, Friendly

Older dapper gent seeks connection for next phase of life. Meet Cash - he's a gentle sweetheart who's looking for the next love of his life following the passing of his previous human. 

Cash is an easy going guy. He's friendly to every adult/child/dog/cat we've met - either politely greeting them or simply ignoring them and letting them be. He's a real people person and likes to follow his favorite gal around the house (he seems to connect more with women we've found - likes men, but a Velcro dog for women), but when left alone he settles onto his dog bed and patiently waits for your return.

Being an older guy he's got things down. He knows when it's potty time or will let you know he needs to head out - he's got a great old man bark. He walks well on leash; no tugging or lunging at small animals. He loves a good car ride - head out the window! He's great in public; we took him to various breweries during his first week here and he couldn't have been better. Being older also has some challenges - he's got some hip pain/arthritis and needs a daily med/supplement. He has trouble getting into a car - we've been introducing him to a ramp, but he's not a huge fan, so there's some lifting in and out.

General day for Cash:
6:30 am: Wake up and first big walk of the. "Big" is relative; we don't go far - he's got some hip issues so we take a nice slow walk. He knows it is time to potty and gets his business done. A few blocks is all he needs.

7am food (and joint supplement/med) He takes his meds very easily with some peanut butter.

Then he'll either go to work with me or we stay home and work from home. Cash is happy to visit the office and see people. Then he'll settle down and let me work. 

12 noon food. And potty break - very short.

More patiently waiting while work is done. A potty break if needed.

5pm: Day is done. Second big walk (again "big" here is pretty small).

Pets and play time, settles near us if we're home. If we go out he's good about being alone.

7pm food (and joint supplement/med)

9:30 pm: Last walk of the night. Upon return - dentastix and bed time. We let him sleep outside the kennel. He did fine in it, but we thought it was uncomfortable for his hips (and he's so well behaved) that we just let him free roam in the family room and sleep on a dog bed by the patio door.

If you're looking for a sweet, older guy to enjoy slow, short walks and nice quiet sitting you gotta meet Cash.

animals.Name: Daisy
animals.Description:
Daisy is a tiny firecracker with a whole lot of attitude and even more love to give! Daisy is the definition of personality:  confident, opinionated, and absolutely convinced the world looks better when she’s in charge of it. She’s playful, clever, and always ready to add a little extra “zing” to your day.

Daisy adores attention, thrives on interaction, and isn’t shy about telling you exactly how she feels - whether that’s demanding snacks, announcing her presence, or reminding you that she is, in fact, the star of the show. Under all that sass though is a sweetheart who bonds deeply and loves fiercely.

She’s looking for a home that appreciates a companion with flair, humor, and a dash of drama. If you’re ready for a lively little sidekick who will keep you laughing and, on your toes, Daisy is your girl.

animals.Name: Fern
animals.Description:
Fern is a bright, spirited little gal who brings joy everywhere she goes. Fern is the perfect blend of sweetness and fun - she loves affection, cozy naps, and being close to her people, but she’s just as excited to play, explore, and turn ordinary moments into tiny adventures. Her goofy charm, love of toys, and curious nature make her a constant source of smiles.

Fern is looking for a loving home that appreciates a companion with both heart and personality. She’s ready to bring warmth, laughter, and a whole lot of life into her new family. If you’re hoping for a friend who will keep you entertained and adored in equal measure, Fern is ready to bloom with you.

animals.Name: Holly
animals.Description:
Holly is the perfect mix of sweet, curious, and delightfully spunky. Holly loves exploring her world, batting at toys, and wiggling her way into your arms - but she’s just as happy to curl up for a warm, cozy nap on your lap.

She’s already showing her affectionate personality, leaning into snuggles and soaking up every bit of love she can get. Whether she’s practicing her playful pounces or melting into your arms for cuddle time, Holly brings sunshine to every moment.

This little sweetheart is ready to grow, learn, and thrive with a family who will cherish her gentle spirit and playful spark. If you’re looking for a tiny companion who will fill your home with love, Holly is waiting to meet you.

animals.Name: Bonsai
animals.Description:
Born on April Fool's Day, Bonsai is anything but a joke. This little guy arrived in the world just two months ago, and he's already busy figuring out everything it has to offer - sights, sounds, smells, and hopefully very soon, a family to call his own.

Bonsai is the kind of puppy who can go from full-speed zoomies to a sleepy, squishy snuggle in the span of about four minutes. He's curious without being overwhelming, playful without being relentless - a genuinely balanced little soul who tends to take his cues from the people around him. That adaptability is rare, and it means Bonsai is likely to thrive in all kinds of homes.

If you've been waiting for a puppy who feels like he was made just for you - Bonsai might be exactly that.

animals.Name: Rue
animals.Description:
Rue is a stunning boy with a soft expression and a heart that wants nothing more than to belong to someone special. He’s young, bright, and full of that irresistible puppy charm - but with a calm, thoughtful side that makes him easy to love.

He is the kind of dog who watches you with quiet curiosity, then breaks into a full‑body wiggle when he realizes you’re there just for him. He’s affectionate, eager to learn, and already showing signs of being a loyal, people‑oriented companion.

Rue is still growing into himself, which makes this the perfect time for him to bond deeply with his new family. With patience, structure, and lots of love, he’s going to blossom into the kind of dog who makes every day better just by being there.

If you’re looking for a loyal companion with a tender heart and a bright future, Rue is ready to meet you.

animals.Name: Basil
animals.Description:
Basil came into the world on April 1st, and while his birthday is famous for tricks, there's nothing tricky about this boy. What you see is what you get: a sweet, curious puppy with a whole lot of life ahead of him and zero idea just how lucky his future family is going to feel.

He's got that wonderful puppy balance - enough energy to keep things interesting, enough calm to curl up and just be. He reads the room well for someone so young, matching the energy around him rather than bulldozing it. Active household? He's in. Quiet evenings on the couch? Also yes. Basil doesn't need the world to revolve around him; he just wants to be part of yours.

animals.Name: Sage
Joe Pye
animals.Description:
Joe Pye is a charming boy with a gentle spirit and a heart that’s all-in on love. His name might come from a wildflower, but this boy is anything but wild - he’s thoughtful, affectionate, and happiest when he’s close to someone who makes him feel safe.

Joe Pye has that perfect mix of puppy curiosity and calm sweetness. He watches the world with soft eyes, approaches new friends with a wagging tail, and melts into you the moment you offer a cuddle. 
He’s the kind of dog who wants to bond deeply and be someone’s steady, loyal companion.

Joe Pye is still young and ready to grow into the amazing dog he’s meant to be. With patience, kindness, and a loving home, he’ll blossom into the kind of companion who makes every day brighter.
If you’re looking for a loyal friend with a tender heart and a calm, loving presence, Joe Pye is ready to meet you.

animals.Name: Rowena
animals.Description:
Meet Rowena - a girl who's already been through more in her first weeks of life than most dogs face in a lifetime and is coming out the other side with her tail held high.

Shortly after Rowena arrived in the world, she was found abandoned, alone, and standing in the middle of the highway, clearly trying to fend for herself. She came to us battling mange, and while she's well on her way to a full recovery, she's still growing her coat back in - a little patchy, a little scruffy, and completely, utterly endearing. She's the kind of puppy who makes you root for her the moment you see her.

But here's the thing about Rowena: she has no idea she's been through anything hard. She isn't timid or withdrawn - she's just a puppy, doing puppy things, soaking up every good moment her foster home has to offer. She's got a lovely balance to her - playful when the moment calls for it, content to settle in and just be when it doesn't. She reads her people well and adapts easily, which means she'll fit into just about any household that's ready to love her.

By the time Rowena finds her forever home, that coat is going to be coming in beautifully - and her adopter will have the joy of watching that transformation happen in real time. A puppy who grows into herself, right before your eyes.

Rowena has already shown us who she is: resilient, sweet, and ready. Now she just needs someone to show her, and her favorite chair, where home is.

animals.Name: Minnie
animals.Description: Minnie was surrendered from a hoarding case where she was neglected. She is the sweetest little cuddle bug and does great with all people, dogs and cats. She is potty trained and crate trained. She is not a typical chihuahua in that she is very calm and quiet, not nervous or barky at all. She has an old CCL tear that is getting fixed on May 29 and will be available for adoption after she recovers.

animals.Name: Penelope
animals.Description:
Penelope is a delightful little poodle mix who would love to join your family, follow you everywhere, and sit on your lap whenever possible.  

Penelope is still full of youthful energy. She loves toys-both playing fetch and chewing them into destruction.  She’s satisfied with 2-3 play sessions and 3-4 walks a day.  She does some pulling on leash, but that usually stops about a quarter of the way into our walk. She has interest in squirrels robins, rabbits and ducks, and will pull to try to get to them, but can be easily persuaded to continue on with the walk.

Penelope is a relatively quiet dog in the house. Our resident dog barks at anything that walks by and she just runs to the window and watches without adding her voice to the commotion. She will bark if she’s on a leash and she can’t get to me or my husband or a random neighbor that she’s sure will be her new best friend. 

Penelope is  doing well with my resident dog who is twice her size.  She will appropriately bark/bite at him when he’s overbearing but then as soon as he backs off, she is fine.  She’s learning how to play with him and hold her own in a game of tug.  She’s met neighborhood dogs on walks and is submissive at first and then joins in the typical dog greeting of butt, sniffing and circling.  We don’t own any cats, but the person she lived with for about a month said she didn’t do well with her cats.

Penelope spent her first year of life in a family with two young children. We do not have young children in our lives, so we have not personally seen her interact with kids. 

Penelope knew her name when she arrived and she knew to do her business outside. She has had no accidents in the house!  Other than that she was a blank slate.  She’s a quick learner and is making progress on sit, lie down, wait, stay, touch, and kennel up.  I see great potential for trick training with a future circus career.  

Penelope likes to jump up and thinks that’s the best way to get attention. We’re working at teaching her to keep all 4 feet on the ground in order to get the pets and attention that she wants.  She’s making a little bit of progress, but it’s so hard to keep all 4 feet on the ground! 

Penelope sleeps quietly in a kennel in our bedroom overnight- though she believes that the night ends between 5-6am!  She stays in a kennel, in our dining room, when we leave the house. She will also stay in that kennel when we are home, which I do for brief periods each day, to teach her that she can be content there even when we are around. She puts up a little bit of racket when she first goes in but then settles nicely. 

Penelope arrived in our care badly matted. We had her groomed this week and she has very short hair now and is even shaved in certain spots. Her ears and head and tail are still long and she still looks adorable. She will require regular grooming and brushing between grooming sessions to prevent matting.  

Penelope is a delight. I have no doubt that she will be a wonderful addition to the lucky family that welcomes her.

Attributes.AttributeName: Has many approved adopters waiting to meet them!

animals.Name: Dallas
animals.Description:
When Dallas rounds the corner, there’s no graceful pitter-patter of little feet; instead, you’ll hear the joyful thump of his duck-like paddles. These unique acoustics can probably be attributed to the increased surface area of his very cute, webbed feet, courtesy of the water-loving lab in him. Given their size, Dallas will also likely grow into a big boy.

Dallas will try his hardest to position himself and his uncoordinated limbs on top of any willing human. He can’t get enough attention, love, and cuddles. This also extends to meeting anyone new. There are no strangers in Dallas’ world, only friends he hasn’t met yet.

Potty training is complete! Ongoing success in this area is helped by a consistent routine and a timely reaction to Dallas’ signals. 

Dallas has also learned that humans are not sentient chew toys. Some teenage mouthiness is still there (for now), but is limited to situations in which he’s overstimulated. For that reason, living with young children may not be ideal. Dallas does respond well to corrections, and his highly food-motivated nature makes him an eager learner. 

He is a champ in his crate and sleeps through the night like a pro. His favorite activity is wrestling and romping around with his big canine brother.  He also loves to go on car rides, especially if they involve a pup cup and/or meeting new friends through the drive-thru window.

Dallas recently underwent surgery for hip dysplasia to better align his hip joint.  This will help allow for more joint stability and help prevent future issues.

If you’re ready for a goofy, loving, slightly chaotic sidekick who’s growing into an amazing dog, Dallas is your guy.

animals.Name: Archer James
animals.Description:
NOT AVAILABLE UNTIL 6/8
IN THE MEANTIME, MEET & GREETS WELCOMED 


Hi! My name is Archer, Archie for short, A.J. for being cool, and Archer James for long. I’m a very loyal guy. I love my people and look for praise and reinforcement as much as possible. I like sniffing... with my nose.... really close....to the point that my nose makes contact with the item to sniff it extra good! I am getting used to a lot of things and noises, so sometimes I jump a little, but once I hear it and you remind me it’s okay, then I’m good. Just some other things I want to share about myself, I like cuddling, napping, going on walks, and getting the zoomies in the backyard. Okay, now my foster humans can talk….

Archer is a very sweet button boy. He is working on potty training and has only had one true accident in the house. We keep a routine of taking him out right when we wake up (around 6:00 am), after breakfast at 7:30 am, and before we leave for work/start working from home. Weather permitting, we take a short walk with our resident doggos before breakfast. He stays in a kennel during the day when home alone. He does cry a little but calms himself down. If working from home, he gets to go out multiple times throughout the day. If in the office, he goes out again around 4:00 pm.

Dinner is between 5:00 pm and 5:30 pm. Followed again by going outside. Sometimes we’ll stay outside and play, get the zoomies, or cuddle inside. We do take a nighttime walk, depending on the weather, either around 7:00 pm or before dinner, around 4:00 pm. He’s learning to walk on a leash, has taken cues from our resident doggos, and is coming leaps and bounds already. Archer is very good at looking up for reassurance and positive reinforcement. He doesn’t quite understand how to go poo on the grass, so he goes on the cement walkway instead. He marked in the house when he first got here; however, we haven’t seen this as a continued concern. He really benefits from quiet time in his kennel; he calms down quickly, gets comfy, and relaxes. It really is a safe space. Sometimes we can leave the door open, and he will just wander in for a nap. 

Some interesting notes: He enjoys ceiling fans, likes to nap under a blanket, and loves to roll in the grass. However, he does NOT like bagpipes or loud cars.

animals.Name: George
animals.Description:
Meet George 🐶

This sweet beagle is looking for a patient, loving home where he can continue building confidence and enjoy life with a furry best friend by his side. He will be available for adoption after 6/8/26.

George is a gentle soul with a soft personality and an even softer spot for other dogs — he loves having canine companionship and truly blossoms around them. His ideal home would include another friendly dog to help him feel secure, playful, and part of the pack.

Despite being a beagle, George is surprisingly quiet and keeps barking to a minimum. He’s also cat friendly and happy to coexist peacefully with feline friends. He’s a lower-energy boy overall who absolutely loves to cuddle, and everyone who meets him comments on how incredibly soft he is. One of George’s cutest qualities is how joyful he becomes outside; upon first seeing grass, and honestly every time after that, he loves to skip and run around the yard with a ball like it’s the happiest moment of his day.

George is still working on potty training and learning that being in a crate is safe.  He’ll continue to make progress with additional patience, routine, and reassurance and will thrive with an adopter who understands that building confidence takes time and who’s willing to celebrate the little victories along the way.

What George offers in return is enormous: sweetness, loyalty, companionship, and the kind of dog love that makes every bit of effort worthwhile.

If you have a kind heart, a steady routine, and a dog looking for a new best friend, George just might be your perfect match. 🐾

animals.Name: Sunday
animals.Description:
Sunday is a typically cute and snuggly puppy but an atypically easy one. She's well on her way to mastering house training, stays close on walks, and enthusiastically enjoys trading treats for tricks, yet eats like a lady.  Sunday sleeps quietly in her crate at night for 7-8 hours, loves her chew toys, and has never met a human or dog that she doesn't love immediately. Sunday is easy to read, telling you when she needs to go out, when she's thirsty, when she's ready to sleep, or fired up to play.

This smarty pants has learned tricks quickly. She's already a pro at crossing the street with Place ("here" with a pointed finger), Sit ("sit" with hand gesture), Stay ("stay" with a stop sign hand), Come ("let's go!" with a fast walk across the street next to me). I'm so proud of her very mature tendencies at only 11 weeks! No accidents so far and gives very clear cues when she needs something.

No idea what breed this little tyke is, but I sure don't see her getting real big. Although I will say she sports a big girl “woof” and not a little dog “arf arf”. Don't let her current size fool you. Her legs are stretching out and she will go with you anywhere any time on any terrain for as long as you want.

I imagine Sunday would be equally thrilled being in a home with only humans or with canine or feline companions.

She still naps around 5-6 times a day intermixed with indoor and lots of outdoor play time. She is most content just hanging out in the yard or surrounded by kitchen activity. She will also make herself available to nap any time, just say the word.

She's currently eating two meals a day with chewy and crunchy treats in between.

If you're looking for a gorgeous, loyal and smart sidekick to grow with, Sunday is your match.

animals.Name: Hannah
animals.Description:
Meet Hannah 🐾

Hannah is a sweet, snuggly puppy who’s well on her way to becoming an amazing companion. She absolutely loves curling up close for cuddles and happily keeps herself busy with a good chew bone.

Her favorite places are the couch and the bed, she would love to sleep with you, but also is fine sleeping near by. Her foster parents don’t let her sleep in bed during the night, so she often chooses the next closest place, sleeping under the bed.

She’s a smart girl who’s eager to learn—she already knows “sit” and is currently working on mastering “down.” Hannah is potty trained, sleeps through the night, and can be left out of a crate without getting into trouble (in fact, she’d prefer to skip the crate altogether!).

Hannah enjoys playing with other dogs and has been doing great with her two foster dog siblings. She’s still a puppy, but she’s growing into a good girl who will make a wonderful addition to a loving home.

If you’re looking for a playful cuddle buddy, Hannah might just be your perfect match.

animals.Name: Ranger
animals.Description:
Ranger is the goofiest guy you might ever meet! He is a rambunctious, curious, truly hilarious, and zany guy. On one hand, he is a TOTAL husky, and on the other... he's completely different!
In husky form: he really does not like to be confined. This guy wants free range of the house, believes he knows what's best at all times, is vocal (you'll never to struggle to understand what he's wanting), and is down for every adventure. He is active and also wildly intelligent.
In non-husky form: He has INCREDIBLE recall off-leash, wants to follow you everywhere, oversee every task (or actually just do it for you?), and most certainly wants to ensure no one ever intrudes!
Ranger has been getting kennel trained, but realistically he hates it (see: total husky). He does not want to be a kennel guy, and is working on building up house manners (and the humans' trust) to be left unkenneled during the day and while sleeping at night. He is mostly house trained and has gotten great at signaling when he needs to go out. He loves to learn new tricks/commands, and he does so quickly! He lives in a house with other dogs and cats, and would really love to live in a house with other dogs when he's adopted, but could be an only dog with the right family. He occasionally BORKS at the cats, but has not shown any interest or drive to try to hurt them, though the same is not true for the mice in the garage or the moles in the yard (though he was very proud to present them to us). He lives in a house with a fenced in yard and while that would also be his preference in a forever home, could also do okay otherwise with the right people. 
Ranger has met several kids at a wide variety of ages and has done well with them. Occasionally in new settings he can be shy or suspicious of new people, and he watches his humans or foster dog siblings for cues on how to behave. 
Ranger's ideal home would be one where he could be active, could have a confident leader (human and/or dog!), and could have his intelligent little brain engaged. He is a loyal, tenacious little guy who can't wait to find his hoomans!

animals.Name: Peaches
animals.Description:
Peaches is a sweet, playful, independent doggo with an adorable fox face and a tomboy streak. She loves playing fetch, tug of war and chasing rabbits around the backyard; sniffing (and marking) throughout the neighborhood. She is smart and food motivated, knows “sit” and “touch” and is working on “wait.”  As is typical for her breed, she is on the more independent side, preferring to come to you to snuggle when she feels like it. That said, she is very attuned with her humans, usually choosing to be near the action and often cuddling up against one of us on the couch or bed. She also enjoys grooming her people, licking and gently nibbling hands, legs and feet if allowed - she knows it’s important to keep her people clean!

Peaches has gotten along well with other dogs and would love to play with most dogs she meets. Her previous owners stated she was cat-friendly, but we haven’t found her to be trustworthy with our cat. She generally warms up to new people quickly, especially if they give her a bit of space as she doesn’t seem to enjoy being petted by new people. She has not met any children while staying with us.

Her day begins with a 20 minute walk followed by breakfast in a puzzle bowl and some cuddle time while one of her humans goes through their morning routine. She is home alone throughout the work day, typically free roaming, and seems fine going 8-10 hours without a potty break. She gets a second walk and some backyard playtime in the evening, followed by dinner. She typically follows us around throughout the evening, wanting to be in the yard if we’re outside, in the basement if we’re doing laundry, on the couch if it’s TV time. She has free range of the common spaces of the house at night (as the cat is with us in the bedroom). We did crate her initially, and she settled well after vocalizing for a couple of minutes. She did initially vocalize quite a bit when separated from us at bedtime, and again in the morning, but has settled very nicely into our routine. We have not seen signs of separation anxiety when we are away from home, only when we are in another room and she does not have access to us. She displays this with loud vocalizations and scratching at doors, but no destructive behaviors. 

You won’t need millions of peaches when you have this one Peaches!

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Ginger
animals.Description:
Ginger is a sweetheart who loves hanging out with her people, going for long walks, and snuggling up in the coziest spots in the house.

In her foster home she lives with a large dog foster brother and 2 young kids and she does great with them. When Ginger gets excited she doesn't have great spatial awareness and may knock over toddlers/babies and people with mobility limitations, but generally she is very calm and gentle. Her interaction with cats is unknown at this time.

Ginger gets anxious during thunderstorms and likes hiding under a bed as her safe spot. She likes quietly observing animals outside and she hardly ever barks. She is working on not jumping when first meeting a person, but listens pretty well to commands. She will sit when getting her leash on/waiting to go outside.

Ginger walks well on a leash, can be left out to free roam inside the house, is potty trained, and does great in the car. She likes occasional play sessions with toys and checking out what everyone is doing, but spends most of her time resting around the house. She thinks she is a lap dog and loves cuddles. Ginger loves exploring and hanging out in the sunshine.

animals.Name: Edmond
animals.Description:
Meet Edmond, a tender‑hearted hound mix who’s discovering the world at his own quiet pace. He’s a shy soul, the kind of dog who watches first, thinks second, and then slowly leans in when he realizes you’re someone he can trust. Once he does, you’ll see the sweetest transformation - soft tail wags, gentle eye contact, and a pup who wants nothing more than to be close to his person.
Edmond’s ideal home is one that understands the beauty of patience. Loud chaos isn’t his style; he thrives in calm spaces with kind humans who let him bloom on his own timeline. He enjoys peaceful walks, cozy corners, and the comfort of a steady routine.
Underneath his timid exterior is a deeply loving dog who just needs someone to show him he’s safe. With time, consistency, and compassion, Edmond will reward his family with quiet loyalty, soft affection, and the kind of bond that feels truly earned.
If you’re looking for a gentle companion who will blossom with love, Edmond is ready to take those first brave steps with you.

animals.Name: Larry
animals.Description: Larry was surrendered from an Amish farm where he was rused for breeding. He was very matted when we got him (see photo) so he had to be completely shaved, but when his hair grows out he will be really cute and fluffy! Larry is shy at first but very sweet. Even when he is scared and cowering away from people, his tail wags nonstop. He loves playing with other dogs, especially his buddy Milo. He previously lived in a kennel building so is just now learning potty training and leash training, but he’s doing so well! He is already almost perfect on a leash and has not had any accidents indoors in a couple weeks. He still has not managed to figure out stairs so he needs to be carried up and down stairs.

animals.Name: Plum
animals.Description:
Say “hi” to Plum.  She’s a peach of a pup! Plum is the best of everything, she’s a world-class snuggler and is just as happy playing independently. Her olive green eyes are mesmerizing. 

Bragging points for Plum:
- sleeps through the night in her crate since she arrived 
- learned “sit” in 2 days
- potty training is progressing nicely; hardly any accidents in the past few days
- enjoys playing and napping with our resident dogs

These are huge wins considering Plum just turned 9 weeks old and has only been away from her siblings for a week. 

Plum’s schedule:
5:30-6:30 am ~ wake up, outside, eat, play
7 am - 10:30 pm ~ combination of crate, naps, play, eat, outside for some stick chewing and shade napping 
10:30 pm ~ kisses goodnight and to the crate she goes

Plum is silly, fun and a pure joy to foster. She’s looking for a lifelong companion, do you fit the bill?

animals.Name: Pancake
animals.Description:
This pancake doesn’t need any syrup to be sweet.  She has been a joy to have in our home.  At almost 4 years old, she is a wonderful mix of playful and calm.  She loves to play fetch with a tennis ball.  She is happy to have someone throw it, but also happy to toss the ball to herself.  She loves a good walk to find new smells, critters to chase, and people to meet.
Currently, Pancake starts her day around 6 with a quick potty break and then in for breakfast by 7.  If you have another dog it is best to feed her separate since she likes to help herself to any food available.  It doesn’t matter if another dog’s head is in the way. She also likes to take treats so should be watched with other dogs that have a problem with that. She usually gets a walk or let outside every 3 hours for a potty break.  When I leave for short trips, I take her in the car.  If I’m gone for hours then she goes in the kennel.  She doesn’t love it but tolerates being put in there.  Last potty break is around 9-9:30. She prefers to sleep outside the kennel at night.  She has slept in our room the past few nights with the door shut.  She doesn’t make a peep.   
She doesn’t bark a lot but will bark some in the kennel or when playing with a doggie friend.
She is great with my 11 and 14 year old kids.  She jumps occasionally and so that might bother a smaller child.  She doesn’t do it often though.  
If you show her love and devote time to her, she will make a great companion for most any person or family.

animals.Name: Fudge
animals.Description:
Fudge is the total puppy package: adorable, social, snuggly, and impressively smart for such a young guy. This clever boy picked up on potty training in just ONE day when kept on a consistent schedule (after all, puppies are trying their best and tiny bladders can only do so much!).
He’s already learning great leash manners and enjoys strolling around to meet new people, dogs, and basically anyone willing to say hello. Fudge has made a few puppy besties already and absolutely adores children — he’s the kind of pup who wants to be part of every family adventure.
Whether he’s making friends, showing off his good-boy skills, or melting hearts with his sweet face, Fudge is ready to bring joy, laughter, and puppy kisses to his forever home. 💛

animals.Name: Bix
animals.Description:
Bix is a chill goofball in fuzzy teddy bear form.

Little man is settling into home life beautifully with a cat, 2 big dogs, and various little humans coming and going.

Potty training has been a dream. We are 99% of the way there already! Bix also goes all night in his crate without a fuss.

He is always ready for big dog adventures and long walks, but in a very portable medium/small dog body. If you can bring him to school drop off or on a Starbucks run, he’s ready to go!

Snuggly, but independent. Sweet, but brave. This little charmer would do well just about anywhere.

animals.Name: Lucas
animals.Description:
The quietest of the siblings, Lucas is the complete package. He is sweet, calm, playful, and calculating. He loves to sit in your lap and watch his siblings play, waiting for the perfect moment to join the fray. 

Brother to Deva, Kai, and Nola!

animals.Name: Pearl
animals.Description:
Pearl was surrendered from an Amish farm where she was used for breeding. She is living in a house for the first time and is blossoming so well! She is very shy at first and will hide from new people but as she gets to know us, she is becoming more and more affectionate. She has never growled or shown any aggression, even when scared. She loves to be around other small dogs but is a little scared of big dogs and will run away from them. She is learning to walk on a leash and potty outside for the first time but she makes progress every day. When indoors, she mostly likes to stay in her pen or crate. We sit with her and give her pets and she will gently lick our hands.

Pearl is a nonshedding dog and will require regular haircuts. She gets carsick in the car so she would prefer not to be your roadtrip buddy. Otherwise, she is a perfect little girl!

animals.Name: Barkeley
animals.Description: Meet and Greets welcome!

animals.Name: Maxasaurus
animals.Description:
Maxasaurus is a big bundle of love with an easy-going personality and the sweetest soul. 

Max is friendly with people of all ages and gets along well with animals of all kinds and sizes. He does great with my resident dog, but he’d also be perfectly happy as an only dog soaking up all the attention. He’s a total patio pup — he loves socializing for a bit and then relaxing in the shade like the polite gentleman he is.

Max gets very excited for walks, so he may pull a little at first (especially if he spots a bunny!), but he settles in nicely after the first few minutes. He is currently very overweight and needs to lose about 30 pounds, so he tires quickly and isn’t up for long hikes right now. Overall, he’s a low-energy guy who prefers leisurely strolls and long naps (specifically on my clothes!).

During the workday, Max is crated with a lunch break and does wonderfully in an apartment setting. He would likely do well free roaming, too.
Because my dog guards furniture, Max doesn’t try to climb up on it, but he absolutely loves leaning in for pets and would happily snuggle given the chance.

Maxasaurus has such a goofy personality and is constantly making hilarious faces that will keep you laughing.
He truly is a gem — a dog looking for a soft place to nap, a few daily strolls, and unconditional love. You can feel how badly he wants to be loved just by the way he looks at you.

Attributes.AttributeName: Can free roam when humans are gone

animals.Name: Alfie (fka Blueberry)
animals.Description:
Alfie is a handsome, intelligent young pup who is looking for the right home to help him grow into the wonderful dog he has the potential to be. Originally adopted as a young puppy, Alfie is believed to be a German Shepherd / Anatolian Shepherd mix, which means he’s a smart, energetic working-type dog who thrives with structure, training, and an experienced handler.

Alfie is very people-oriented and loves being near his humans. When he’s calm, he enjoys snuggling and being part of whatever his people are doing. He is friendly and enthusiastic when meeting new people and is always excited to make new friends.

This bright boy has already started learning his basic commands in puppy training classes and has been doing very well. He is eager to please and responds well to consistent training. Alfie is crate-trained overnight and is doing well with potty training. He does well with other dogs and enjoys their company. He has interacted appropriately with dogs in puppy class and is excited to see dogs in the neighborhood.

Alfie is still a young puppy and is very energetic and curious. Like many shepherd-type dogs, he needs both physical exercise and mental enrichment to help him stay balanced and happy.

Because he is still learning impulse control, Alfie can be mouthy during play and may nip at clothing or hands when he becomes overexcited. He will need a patient home committed to continuing training to help him develop appropriate play and manners as he matures. Alfie has also shown resource guarding with high-value chews, such as bully sticks. A home familiar with training and managing this behavior will help him succeed.

Alfie has lived with a cat but has recently begun nipping at and grabbing the cat’s tail, so he will need a home without cats.

Because of his mouthy play style and energy level, Alfie would do best in a home with adults or older, dog-savvy children. A calmer household that can provide structure, training, and consistent leadership will help him thrive.

Alfie’s Ideal Home

    Experience with shepherd or guardian breeds preferred

    Committed to continued training and structure

    Plenty of exercise and mental enrichment

    No cats

    No young children

Alfie is a smart, loyal pup with a lot of potential. In the right home with guidance and consistency, he can grow into a wonderful companion.

animals.Name: Sugar
animals.Description:
Sugar is looking for a new home because she does not get along with the neighbor’s dog and it is unsafe for them to live near each other. It is unclear why she doesn’t like this particular dog because she is typically very friendly and submissive with other dogs. She goes to doggy daycare and boarding and has never had an issue with any of the dogs there.

Hi. Yes, hello. I’m the dog.

My name is Sugar, and apparently I’m writing my own profile because my human says I’m looking for a new home — preferably one with soft blankets, snacks, and humans who appreciate world-class cuddling.

About Me: 
Strong (emotionally and physically
Loyal to my people
The sweetest girl — cuddling is basically my full-time job
Excellent at reminding humans they should go outside more
Professional greeter who acts like you’ve been gone for years (even if you just got groceries)

My Personality Resume:
Loves MOST dogs
Super sweet with kids and all strangers — people are my favorite
Never growled at or been afraid of humans
No chewing your stuff or counter surfing (I have manners)
Comfortable in my kennel, but will happily sleep under the blankets if invited
Takes neighborhood wildlife patrol very seriously (squirrels, birds, rabbits beware)
Cats and I are not compatible roommates

What I’m Looking For:
• A confident human comfortable handling a strong dog
• Someone who enjoys walks, fresh air, and unconditional love
• A home where I can be part of the family (and possibly the bed)
